paper.title: Discovery of a runaway star likely ejected by a Type Iax Supernova

review.summary: Six internal tables: Gaia DR3 astrometry of the target star J1022-3414 (tab:kinematics), stellar atmospheric parameters and abundance ratios from FORS2 spectroscopy (tab:abundances), MCMC posterior summary for the flat-magnitude prior (tab:stats), orbital and kinematic parameters including ejection velocity and boundness (tab:kinematicparams), MCMC summary for the distance prior (tab:stats2), and MCMC summary for the mass-prior case (tab:stats3). No concrete external data-set URLs are provided in the source.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Presents the Gaia DR3 astrometric parameters of the hypervelocity candidate J1022-3414, used for target selection and kinematic modelling.

columns.name: Parameter
columns.meaning: Name of the astrometric or photometric parameter

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Summarises the stellar atmospheric parameters (Teff, log g, vrad) and elemental abundance ratios relative to oxygen derived from FORS2 spectroscopy, used to constrain the evolutionary origin of J1022-3414.

source_refs.caption: Stellar atmospheric parameters and abundance ratios.
source_refs.label: tab:abundances

columns.name: Parameter
columns.meaning: Name of the atmospheric or abundance parameter

columns.name: Value
columns.meaning: Measured or derived value with uncertainty

internal_tables.evidence: Stellar atmospheric parameters and abundance ratios.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Summarises the posterior distributions of distance, absolute magnitude, mass, radius, ejection velocity, and midplane-crossing time sampled with emcee under a flat absolute-magnitude prior.

source_refs.caption: Summary of parameters sampled with emcee with mode, median, and 16--84\% confidence intervals.
source_refs.label: tab:stats

columns.name: Parameters
columns.meaning: Name of the sampled stellar or kinematic parameter

internal_tables.evidence: Summary of parameters sampled with emcee with mode, median, and 16--84\% confidence intervals.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Lists the present-day and Galactic-plane-crossing orbital parameters (position, velocity, boundness, flight time) of J1022-3414, derived from kinematic integration with 1-sigma confidence intervals.

source_refs.caption: Orbital parameters for \gstar. Quoted uncertainties correspond to the 1$\sigma$ confidence intervals.
source_refs.label: tab:kinematicparams

columns.name: Parameter
columns.meaning: Name of the orbital or kinematic parameter

columns.name: Parameter
columns.meaning: Name of the corresponding future/past parameter at plane crossing

columns.name: Value
columns.meaning: Value at plane crossing with uncertainty

internal_tables.evidence: Orbital parameters for \gstar. Quoted uncertainties correspond to the 1$\sigma$ confidence intervals.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Same as tab:stats but using a distance-based prior instead of a flat absolute-magnitude prior, showing how the inferred distance, mass, and ejection velocity shift.

source_refs.caption: Summary of parameters sampled with \texttt{emcee} with mode, median, and 16--84\% confidence intervals.
source_refs.label: tab:stats2

columns.name: Parameter
columns.meaning: Name of the sampled stellar or kinematic parameter

internal_tables.evidence: Summary of parameters sampled with \texttt{emcee} with mode, median, and 16--84\% confidence intervals.

internal_tables.role_in_paper: Same as tab:stats but imposing a conservative upper mass limit of 0.5 solar masses, which narrows the ejection velocity range and strengthens the bound hypervelocity-star interpretation.

source_refs.caption: Summary of stellar parameters with mode, median, and 16--84\% confidence intervals, assuming a $<0.5$ \msun\, prior on mass.
source_refs.label: tab:stats3

columns.name: Parameter
columns.meaning: Name of the sampled stellar or kinematic parameter

internal_tables.evidence: Summary of stellar parameters with mode, median, and 16--84\% confidence intervals, assuming a $<0.5$ \msun\, prior on mass.
